sections.criterion: Quality and efficiency of the implementation

consistency_log.description: Objective consistency across Excellence, Impact, and Implementation sections
WP reference consistency: all WP IDs cited in Excellence and Impact exist in the canonical WP list (WP1–WP9) from Implementation wp_table_refs
Partner name consistency: all partner names across sections match Tier 3 legal_name or short_name; legal entity suffixes (AG, Oy) verified present wherever full legal name is used
Deliverable ID consistency: deliverable IDs and their described purposes are consistent across sections
consistency_log.inconsistency_note: D8-02 is described as 'MAESTRO benchmark suite dataset, month 42' (WP8, FIIT) in Excellence B.1.2, but is grouped under 'AI-on-demand platform contributions (D8-02, D9-02)' in Impact B.2.1 without identifying it as the benchmark dataset artifact. These are different primary purposes attributed to the same deliverable ID. While the benchmark suite may be contributed to the AI-on-demand platform, Impact B.2.1 does not make this link explicit, creating an evaluator-visible attribution inconsistency. Resolution required: Impact B.2.1 should clarify that D8-02 is the MAESTRO benchmark suite dataset (WP8, FIIT, M42) that constitutes the AI-on-demand platform contribution.

consistency_log.description: Milestone ID consistency: milestone IDs referenced in Impact and Implementation are consistent
KPI consistency: quantified targets in Impact section are complete relative to Tier 3 measurable_targets and consistent with Excellence; no partial metric loss detected
Impact claim grounding: each impact claim in the Impact section cites a WP or deliverable mechanism present in the Implementation section wp_table_refs
Budget and resource claim consistency: no explicit budget figures or person-month allocations appear in any section content; no cross-section budget inconsistency possible
Terminology consistency: canonical component/system names from Tier 3 objective titles used with their component keywords across all sections
consistency_log.inconsistency_note: Tier 3 canonical title for OBJ-3 is 'Decentralised multi-agent coordination protocols'; 'protocols' is a listed component keyword. The stem 'Decentralised multi-agent coordination' appears WITHOUT the keyword in: Implementation B.3.1 ('WP4 (Decentralised Multi-Agent Coordination, CERIA, M3–M36)'), Implementation B.3.2 ('leads WP4 (Decentralised Multi-Agent Coordination)'), and Impact B.2.1 ('CERIA's Decentralised Multi-Agent Coordination (WP4, D4-02)'). The full canonical name appears only in Excellence B.1.1. Per strengthened validation, stem appearing without component keyword in two sections must be flagged. Resolution: Impact B.2.1 and Implementation B.3.1/B.3.2 references to WP4 should append 'Protocols' to align with the canonical Tier 3 objective title.

consistency_log.description: Section ordering and completeness: all three Part B sections present and ordered per HE RIA/IA application form template (Excellence=1, Impact=2, Implementation=3)
Traceability footer consistency: all sections have non-empty primary_sources arrays; no section combines no_unsupported_claims_declaration=true with overall_status=unresolved
Validation status aggregation: overall_status recorded for each section; unresolved statuses flagged for downstream review
consistency_log.inconsistency_note: Informational: Excellence overall_status=inferred (TRL progression claim inferred; all eight OBJ-level claims confirmed); Impact overall_status=inferred (EI-04 EU strategic autonomy mapping inferred from destination placement only, per CS-06); Implementation overall_status=confirmed (all 15 claims confirmed against phase outputs and Tier 3). No section has overall_status=unresolved. No blocking downstream gate concern from validation status alone.

traceability_footer.derivation_note: Assembled draft inherits section-level proposal claim traceability from referenced section artifacts; this artifact adds only section index and cross-section consistency findings.
